app_category_descriptions.ai: AI apps for assistants, simulations, and creative thinking.
app_category_descriptions.all: Every routable Tuturuuu app available from this launcher.
app_category_descriptions.content: Content apps for publishing, files, and delivery.
app_category_descriptions.core: Core entrypoints for the Tuturuuu workspace platform.
app_category_descriptions.developer: Developer utilities, gateways, and technical tools.
app_category_descriptions.learning: Learning apps for courses, practice, and teaching.
app_category_descriptions.miscellaneous: Miscellaneous apps for docs, utilities, and public shortcuts.
app_category_descriptions.operations: Operations apps for money, inventory, and commerce.
app_category_descriptions.productivity: Productivity apps for schedule, tasks, messages, and meetings.

teach.familyBody: Publish handoff notes that make sense beside Learn progress, parent views, and the central workspace record.
teach.footer: Teach stays light: plan in context, manage in Tuturuuu, and send learners to Learn.
teach.handoffBody: Open learner previews with the same education workspace context instead of guessing what students will see.
teach.heroLead: Teach is the teacher-facing app for course user groups, classroom signals, and the daily loop between modules, attendance, reports, and metrics.
teach.heroPanelBody: Public landing stays here. After sign-in, teachers enter a workspace dashboard backed by the central Tuturuuu account.
teach.planBody: Sketch the next class loop, then jump into apps/web for the real workspace objects.
teach.signalBody: Keep attention on what needs teaching action, not another dashboard copy.
teach.studioBody: Course user groups, modules, attendance, reports, and metrics stay framed as one teaching workspace.
teach.syncBody: Teach forwards protected API traffic to apps/web so workspace ownership stays centralized.

teachDashboard.emptyGroupsBody: Create workspace user groups in apps/web first. Teach will present each group as a course group with modules, attendance, reports, and metrics.

attendance.body: Open the centralized attendance surface for the selected workspace groups.
attendance.title: Attendance pulse

groups.body: Use workspace user groups as course cohorts with a clear teaching owner.
groups.title: Course groups

metrics.body: Review score metrics before they become learner and parent-facing progress.
metrics.title: Metrics lab

modules.body: Keep lesson sequence, module coverage, and next steps tied to the group.
modules.title: Module map

preview.body: Open the learner app to inspect how the course path appears outside Teach.
preview.title: Learner preview

reports.body: Prepare reporting loops from the same groups, attendance, and score metrics.
reports.title: Report studio

featureGrid.lead: Six high-frequency teaching paths stay visible so Teach feels like a classroom operations dashboard, not a landing page.
featureGrid.title: Course group command center

teachDashboard.groupsLead: In apps/web these are workspace user groups. In Teach, treat each one as a course group with learners, modules, attendance, reports, and metrics.
teachDashboard.lead: Manage the teaching loop without turning Teach into a second platform admin. The dashboard focuses on groups, modules, centralized attendance, reports, and score metrics.

attendance.body: Reconcile attendance with the current course group before class context goes stale.
attendance.title: Attendance queue

metrics.body: Review the score metrics that will shape learner progress and parent-facing reports.
metrics.title: Metrics review

plan.body: Check module coverage and prepare the next lesson sequence for each active course group.
plan.title: Module planning

reports.body: Prepare report notes while the group status, attendance, and scores are still nearby.
reports.title: Report prep

teachDashboard.operationsLead: A focused board for the next teaching actions that usually happen after opening course groups.
teachDashboard.title: Run today’s teaching dashboard.

attendance.body: Use centralized attendance for the group, then keep the result tied to the same workspace record.
attendance.title: Check attendance once

groups.body: Start with workspace user groups and read them as dedicated course groups inside Teach.
groups.title: Own the course group

metrics.body: Scores in the classroom map to platform metrics, so reporting stays consistent across Teach, Learn, and apps/web.
metrics.title: Review score metrics

modules.body: Attach the lesson sequence to the group so students see the right module path in Learn.
modules.title: Sequence modules

teachDashboard.realtimeLead: Top 5 active courses — students, pending grading, unsubmitted work, and low scorers at a glance.
teachDashboard.statsUnavailableBody: Try again shortly to see pending grading, missing submissions, and low-scorer alerts.

settings-account.unlink-confirmation: Are you sure you want to unlink your {provider} account? You will no longer be able to sign in using this account.
settings-account.use-strong-passwords-tip: Use strong, unique passwords for all accounts

settings-account.workspace-activity-description: Notifications about workspace changes and updates

audit-log-insights.detail_description: Inspect the exact before and after values for this audit event.
audit-log-insights.activity_description: Review how user-record changes clustered across the selected period.
audit-log-insights.description: Inspect who changed workspace user records during {period}, narrow the event stream with focused filters, and export the exact slice you are reviewing.
audit-log-insights.export_description: Export the filtered audit events for {period}.
audit-log-insights.no_activity: No audit events were recorded for this selection.
audit-log-insights.repair_description: Preview archive-related status rows that can be reconstructed from the backup audit records, then run the one-time backfill when you are ready.
audit-log-insights.repair_preview_count: {count} missing status-history rows are ready to backfill.
audit-log-insights.repair_preview_empty: No missing archive-related status rows were found.
audit-log-insights.repair_preview_row: User {userId} will be restored into status history as {source}.
audit-log-insights.table_description: Review each normalized workspace-user audit event for the selected period, then open any row to inspect its changed fields.
audit-log-insights.title: Workspace user audit

audit-log-table.field_changes_description: Only fields that changed in this audit event are shown here.

image_upload.file_too_large: File is too large. Maximum size is {size}.

user-data-table.error_loading_emails_description: There was an error loading the sent emails. Please try again.
user-data-table.no_permission_to_refer: You don't have permission to refer users
user-data-table.no_sent_emails_description: This user has not received any emails yet.
user-data-table.refer_summary: Each referral gives you {percent}% discount, capped after {cap} referrals.
user-data-table.referral_settings_desc: Set the referral discount cap and discount increment to enable referrals for this workspace.
user-data-table.search_person_to_refer_placeholder: Search for a person to refer...

guest_lead_threshold.description: Modify the minimum amount of date a user has to attend to be elligible for a follow up email

guest_leads.create_settings_tooltip: Create guest lead settings to enable lead generation.
guest_leads.description: Manage guest user leads and follow-up emails in your workspace.

ws-members.delete-member-description: You can delete this member from the workspace.

ws-user-linked-coupons.empty_description: Link a coupon to the user. The best coupon will be applied automatically at checkout.

ws-user-profile-links.create_description: Choose which fields the recipient may complete.
ws-user-profile-links.create_email_hint: Email is locked to the recipient's logged-in account email and cannot be set to anything else.
ws-user-profile-links.create_fields_tooltip: Only selected fields can be submitted through this link. Any other submitted fields are rejected.
ws-user-profile-links.create_prefill_existing_values_descripti: Keep this on when the recipient should review and correct the current profile details.
ws-user-profile-links.create_prefill_existing_values_tooltip: When disabled, the form starts blank so the recipient cannot see the existing values for the selected fields.
ws-user-profile-links.create_require_auth_description: Recipient must sign in; their account email is used and locked.
ws-user-profile-links.create_require_auth_off_description: Anyone with the link can submit without an account. Limit abuse with an expiry and a max number of uses.
ws-user-profile-links.create_require_auth_tooltip: When on, the recipient must sign in before completing the form, and their account email is used and locked. Turn off to let anyone with the link submit without an account.
ws-user-profile-links.create_success_hint: Share this link with the recipient to collect their details.
ws-user-profile-links.manager_description: Share links that let people complete their own profile details after logging in.
ws-user-profile-links.select_target_user_description: Choose the existing profile that this link should update.
ws-user-profile-links.target_missing: This link's target user could not be loaded.

ws-users.address_tooltip: The physical address of this user.
ws-users.archived_at_tooltip: Set a date when this user will be temporarily archived. After this date passes, the user will be automatically archived.
ws-users.archived_user_tooltip: Permanently archive this user. Archived users cannot be assigned to new tasks.
ws-users.birthday_tooltip: The birth date of this user.
ws-users.delete_confirmation: Are you sure you want to delete this user? This action cannot be undone.
ws-users.delete_warning: Warning: All related data associated with this user will be permanently deleted.
ws-users.description: Users are people who are directly or indirectly related to your workspace. They can be assigned to tasks, projects, and more.
ws-users.display_name_tooltip: This name will be displayed everywhere in the current workspace for this user.
ws-users.email_tooltip: The email address for this user.
ws-users.ethnicity_tooltip: The ethnic background of this user.
ws-users.export_progress_hint: The export scans every matching user, including records outside the current table page.
ws-users.export_scope_description: Export every row that matches the filters below, not just the current page.
ws-users.export_scope_hint: Use the status selector here when you need archived guest records in the same file.
ws-users.export_status_scope_hint: Choose whether the file should include active users only or archived users as well.
ws-users.full_name_tooltip: The real name of this user.
ws-users.gender_tooltip: The gender identity of this user.
ws-users.guardian_tooltip: The guardian or parent information for this user.
ws-users.guest_user_tooltip: If enabled, the user will be linked to the workspace's guest group.
ws-users.national_id_tooltip: The national identification number for this user.
ws-users.note_tooltip: Additional notes or comments about this user.
ws-users.phone_tooltip: The phone number for this user.
ws-users.platform_link_repair_failed_description: The link repair could not be completed. Please try again.
ws-users.platform_link_repair_result_summary: {linked, plural, one {# profile linked} other {# profiles linked}}; {skipped, plural, one {# skipped} other {# skipped}}.
ws-users.user_id_tooltip: The identification number of this user in your workspace. This is automatically managed by Tuturuuu, and cannot be changed.
ws-users.both_linked_cluster_warning: This cluster contains users linked to different platform accounts. They cannot be merged until one is unlinked.
ws-users.both_linked_warning: Both users are linked to different platform accounts. Merging would cause one platform user to lose their workspace association. Please unlink one user first.
ws-users.bulk_merge_confirm_description: Are you sure you want to merge all {count} duplicate clusters? This action cannot be undone.
ws-users.collision_warning_description: During the merge operation, some records had to be removed because both users had conflicting data in the same tables. This can happen when both users have records that cannot coexist (e.g., same invoice, same attendance date).
ws-users.collision_warning_note: The merge was successful, but you may want to review the affected data. The removed records belonged to the source user that was merged.
ws-users.duplicate_detect_failed: Failed to detect duplicates. Please try again.
ws-users.duplicate_idle_description: Click the button below to scan for duplicate users based on matching email or phone numbers.
ws-users.duplicate_merge_failed: Failed to merge users. Please try again.
ws-users.duplicate_select_target: Select which user to keep (the other will be merged into it):
ws-users.duplicates_description: Detect and merge duplicate users based on matching email or phone number
ws-users.feedback_queue_description: {count, plural, =0 {No users currently require attention} one {# user currently requires attention} other {# users currently require attention}}
ws-users.feedback_records_description: {count, plural, =0 {No feedback records yet} one {# feedback record available} other {# feedback records available}}
ws-users.feedback_support_description: Track follow-up notes, cross-group context, and require-attention flags for this user.
ws-users.merge_collision_occurred: Merge completed with data conflicts. Some records were removed.
ws-users.merge_confirm_description: You are about to merge "{source}" into "{target}". All data from the source user will be transferred and the source user will be deleted. This action cannot be undone.
ws-users.merge_confirm_description_multiple: You are about to merge {count} users into "{target}". All data from the source users will be transferred and they will be deleted. This action cannot be undone.
ws-users.merge_warning: This action cannot be undone. The source user will be deleted.
ws-users.platform_link_repair_description: Scan virtual-only workspace profiles and link them to matching platform users that are already workspace members. This never creates workspace membership and does not merge duplicate profiles.
ws-users.platform_link_repair_more_skipped: {count, plural, one {# more skipped profile} other {# more skipped profiles}}
